refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, operator ID, and mission context; (c) all updates are logged with before/after values and justification; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record is complete and auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance targets as a baseline with full context (timestamp, operator, mission ID, environmental conditions).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement and calibration updates with audit trail (who, when, why, before/after values).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure traceability from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record → operational decision.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters (via Tasking interface), applies deterministic algorithms to compute performance targets, records baselines in a versioned database, and exposes a query/audit API.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Local Caching – Performance derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; each computes targets locally and syncs to a central audit log via Telemetry.
DP-C: Manual Baseline Entry with Validation – Site Manager manually enters performance targets via a UI form; system validates against mission context and records with audit metadata.
DP-D: Hybrid Automated + Manual Override – Centralized engine derives targets automatically; Site Manager can override with justification; all changes logged.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid Automated + Manual Override – A centralized Performance Derivation Engine automatically computes performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA) using deterministic algorithms. Site Manager and Tele-operator can review and override targets with explicit justification. All derivations, baselines, and updates are recorded in a versioned audit log with full traceability (timestamp, actor, mission context, before/after values, justification). The engine exposes a Tasking interface for mission input and a Telemetry interface for baseline/history export.

potential_sources: DP-D couples the derivation logic to mission context (Tasking input) and audit logging (Telemetry output), but these are well-defined external interfaces and do not create hidden dependencies within the design domain.
The hybrid approach (automatic + manual override) introduces a decision point (when to override) that must be governed by policy, but this is a control flow dependency, not a design coupling.
No circular dependencies detected: mission context → derivation → baseline → audit log is a unidirectional flow.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is preferred over DP-B (distributed) because it centralizes the derivation logic, reducing diffusion and ensuring consistency. DP-C (manual only) is rejected because it does not meet the 'automatic derivation' requirement. DP-A (automatic only) is rejected because it lacks the override capability needed for operational flexibility and safety-critical adjustments.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available via Tasking interface in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON schema with terrain, payload, cycle count, energy budget).
ASSUMPTION: Site Manager and Tele-operator have authority and training to override performance targets with justification.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation engine may produce invalid targets; mitigation: validation rules and fallback to manual entry.
RISK: Override justification may be insufficient or falsified; mitigation: audit trail review by Safety Manager and Maintenance/Quality.

RISK: Derivation algorithms may not account for all safety-critical factors (e.g., operator fatigue, environmental hazards); mitigation: Safety Manager reviews all baselines before operational deployment.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the derivation algorithm specification (e.g., which mission parameters drive which performance targets) and define the override approval workflow (e.g., Safety Manager sign-off required for safety-critical overrides). Add explicit requirement for algorithm validation and periodic recalibration.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Performance Derivation Engine (DP-D) is assigned to NGHE Solution as the system-level capability that orchestrates mission context ingestion, target derivation, baseline recording, and audit logging. This placement ensures that performance requirement management is a system-level concern, not delegated to individual subsystems, and that all stakeholders (Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality) have visibility and control.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA, safety constraints) from Site Manager or mission planning system via Tasking interface. This input drives the performance derivation algorithm.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exports derived performance baselines, update history, and audit logs to Telemetry interface for consumption by Maintenance/Quality, Safety Manager, and external reporting systems. Telemetry also carries operational performance data (actual cycle time, energy consumption, availability) for comparison against baselines.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services (via SafetyAlert) that may trigger re-derivation of performance targets (e.g., if a hazard is detected, availability SLA may be reduced). NGHE Solution also sends derived safety margins and baseline changes to Safety Services for validation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems for mission context relay and baseline distribution. Also used for remote audit log access by Maintenance/Quality and Safety Manager.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level boundary for performance requirement management. The derivation engine, baseline database, and audit log are all system-level concerns that must be visible to all stakeholders. Containment is high because the DP (hybrid automated + manual override) is a cohesive, self-contained capability. Diffusion risk is minimal: only external interfaces are Tasking (in), Telemetry (out), SafetyAlert (inout), and Network Link (inout), all of which are well-defined and necessary.

Vehicle is a subsystem focused on physical actuation and local sensing. Assigning performance derivation to Vehicle would scatter the logic across multiple subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and create tight coupling between mission planning and vehicle control. Not recommended.

Teleoperation is a subsystem focused on remote operator control and situational awareness. While Tele-operator may override performance targets, the derivation engine and baseline management are not core to teleoperation. Assigning to Teleoperation would create diffusion and obscure the system-level nature of performance requirement management.

Safety Services is responsible for safety monitoring and alerts. While Safety Manager reviews and approves performance baselines, the derivation engine itself is not a safety service; it is a system-level capability that Safety Services consumes. Assigning to Safety Services would conflate safety validation with performance derivation.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System Engineering team or Performance Management subsystem within NGHE Solution)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Performance Derivation Engine is a system-level capability that must be owned by NGHE Solution to ensure: (1) centralized, consistent derivation logic; (2) visibility and traceability across all stakeholders; (3) integration with mission planning (Tasking), operational monitoring (Telemetry), and safety validation (SafetyAlert); (4) audit and compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies. Containment in NGHE Solution is justified because the DP is cohesive, the external interfaces are well-defined, and the capability is not a core function of any subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution is too large or poorly structured, the Performance Derivation Engine may become a 'dumping ground' for system-level logic and lose clear ownership. MITIGATION: Define a dedicated Performance Management subsystem or module within NGHE Solution with clear interfaces and responsibilities.

RISK: If Tasking interface is not well-defined or mission context is incomplete, the derivation engine may produce invalid targets. MITIGATION: Validate Tasking input against a schema and provide fallback to manual entry.

RISK: If Telemetry interface is overloaded with too much data, audit logs may become unwieldy and hard to analyze. MITIGATION: Define a separate Audit Log interface or use structured logging with filtering/query capabilities.

RISK: If Safety Manager does not have timely access to baseline updates, safety validation may lag operational deployment. MITIGATION: Implement real-time SafetyAlert notifications for baseline changes and require explicit sign-off before operational use.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define a dedicated Performance Management subsystem or module within NGHE Solution to own the derivation engine, baseline database, and audit log. Clarify the Tasking interface schema (mission context structure) and the Telemetry interface format (baseline and audit log export). Establish a baseline approval workflow with explicit sign-off by Safety Manager and Maintenance/Quality before operational deployment.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (Hybrid Automated + Manual Override) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) automatic derivation from mission context (Tasking input), (2) baseline recording with full context, (3) managed history with audit trail, (4) traceability from worksite input to operational decision. The DP is specific, implementable, and testable. No ambiguity or missing logic detected.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): Performance Derivation Engine is assigned to NGHE Solution, which is the top-level system boundary. This is appropriate because the capability is system-level and must be visible to all stakeholders. However, if NGHE Solution is poorly structured, the engine may become diffused across multiple subsystems. MITIGATION: Define a dedicated Performance Management subsystem within NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment score for NGHE Solution is 95/100, indicating strong alignment. The DP is cohesive and self-contained, with well-defined external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No hidden dependencies or coupling detected. The IM boundary is clear and appropriate for the system architecture.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible via: (1) functional testing of derivation algorithm against mission context inputs; (2) inspection of baseline records and audit logs for completeness and traceability; (3) integration testing with Tasking, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert interfaces; (4) operational testing with Site Manager and Tele-operator overrides; (5) audit trail review by Maintenance/Quality and Safety Manager. All verification methods are standard and implementable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. Sub-FRs are allocated to specific functions within the Performance Derivation Engine (derivation algorithm, baseline recording, history management, traceability). Ownership and responsibility are clear.

recommended_revisions: Clarify the derivation algorithm specification (e.g., which mission parameters drive which performance targets, how safety margins are computed, how energy efficiency is estimated).
Define the Tasking interface schema (mission context structure) and the Telemetry interface format (baseline and audit log export).
Establish a baseline approval workflow with explicit sign-off by Safety Manager and Maintenance/Quality before operational deployment.
Define the override approval process (e.g., when is override allowed, who can approve, what justification is required).
Specify the audit log retention policy and access control (e.g., who can view, modify, or delete audit logs).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR has four sub-FRs that must be individually designed and allocated: (1) derivation algorithm, (2) baseline recording, (3) history management, (4) traceability. Each sub-FR has distinct design parameters and implementation concerns. Proceeding to the next level of decomposition is necessary to ensure completeness and clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA). DP: Deterministic derivation algorithm with parameterized formulas for each target. IM: Performance Derivation Engine (within NGHE Solution).

CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance targets as a baseline with full context (timestamp, operator, mission ID, environmental conditions). DP: Baseline recording module with structured data model and versioning. IM: Baseline Database (within NGHE Solution).

CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement and calibration updates with audit trail (who, when, why, before/after values). DP: Audit log with immutable records and query API. IM: Audit Log Service (within NGHE Solution).

CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure traceability from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record → operational decision. DP: Traceability matrix and cross-reference mechanism. IM: Traceability Service (within NGHE Solution).

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available via Tasking interface in a structured, machine-readable format.
Site Manager and Tele-operator have authority and training to override performance targets with justification.
Safety Manager has authority to approve or reject performance baselines before operational deployment.
Maintenance/Quality has access to audit logs for compliance and quality assurance.
NGHE Solution can be structured with a dedicated Performance Management subsystem or module.
open_risks: Derivation algorithm may not account for all safety-critical factors; mitigation: Safety Manager review and validation.
Override justification may be insufficient or falsified; mitigation: audit trail review and approval workflow.
Tasking interface may not provide complete mission context; mitigation: validation rules and fallback to manual entry.
Audit logs may become unwieldy and hard to analyze; mitigation: structured logging and query capabilities.
Performance targets may become stale or inconsistent with operational reality; mitigation: periodic recalibration and comparison with actual performance data.
evidence_needed: Derivation algorithm specification with parameterized formulas for each performance target.
Tasking interface schema (mission context structure) and Telemetry interface format (baseline and audit log export).
Baseline approval workflow and override approval process.
Audit log retention policy and access control.
Traceability matrix linking worksite inputs to derived requirements to baseline records to operational decisions.
Validation and testing plan for derivation algorithm and baseline recording.
Integration testing plan for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces.
